verses.1: The words of King Lemuel—the revelation which his mother taught him:
verses.3: Don’t give your strength to women,
verses.4: It is not for kings, Lemuel,
verses.5: lest they drink, and forget the law,
verses.6: Give strong drink to him who is ready to perish,
verses.7: Let him drink, and forget his poverty,
verses.8: Open your mouth for the mute,
verses.10: Who can find a worthy woman?
verses.11: The heart of her husband trusts in her.
verses.12: She does him good, and not harm,
verses.14: She is like the merchant ships.
verses.15: She rises also while it is yet night,
verses.16: She considers a field, and buys it.
verses.17: She arms her waist with strength,
verses.18: She perceives that her merchandise is profitable.
verses.19: She lays her hands to the distaff,
verses.20: She opens her arms to the poor;
verses.21: She is not afraid of the snow for her household,
verses.22: She makes for herself carpets of tapestry.
verses.23: Her husband is respected in the gates,
verses.24: She makes linen garments and sells them,
verses.25: Strength and dignity are her clothing.
verses.26: She opens her mouth with wisdom.
verses.27: She looks well to the ways of her household,
verses.28: Her children rise up and call her blessed.
verses.30: Charm is deceitful, and beauty is vain;
verses.31: Give her of the fruit of her hands!
